steps.title: Read the original center
steps.content:
The equation
$$x^2+(y-2)^2=36$$
is in circle form, so circle A has center $(0,2)$ and radius $6$.

steps.title: Shift the center down
steps.content:
Shifting down $4$ units changes the center's y-coordinate from $2$ to $-2$:
$$2-4=-2$$
The new center is $(0,-2)$, and the radius is unchanged.

steps.title: Write the shifted circle
steps.content:
A circle with center $(0,-2)$ and radius $6$ has equation
$$x^2+(y+2)^2=36$$
The answer is A.
